66315f61ddd20e7cbb31dda5.question: This composer warned against the dangers of gambling in his madrigal The Game of Primiera. This composer's madrigal La Caccia is often paired with a madrigal comedy he co-wrote with Cipriano de Rore called The Gossip of Women at the Laundry. After traveling to a foreign country to meet its "virtuosos in the profession of music", this composer showed them his Missa sopra Ecco sì beato giorno, which had a 60-voice Agnus Dei. Giulio Caccini succeeded this composer as the principal musician at the (*) Medici court. This composer's son of the same name wrote the libretto to Monteverdi's L'Orfeo. This composer's trip to England allegedly inspired the Duke of Norfolk to commission a piece for eight choirs of five voices each. For 10 points, name this composer whose motet Ecce Beatam Lucem may have inspired Thomas Tallis to write Spem in Alium.
66315f61ddd20e7cbb31dda5.question_sanitized: This composer warned against the dangers of gambling in his madrigal The Game of Primiera. This composer's madrigal La Caccia is often paired with a madrigal comedy he co-wrote with Cipriano de Rore called The Gossip of Women at the Laundry. After traveling to a foreign country to meet its "virtuosos in the profession of music", this composer showed them his Missa sopra Ecco si beato giorno, which had a 60-voice Agnus Dei. Giulio Caccini succeeded this composer as the principal musician at the (*) Medici court. This composer's son of the same name wrote the libretto to Monteverdi's L'Orfeo. This composer's trip to England allegedly inspired the Duke of Norfolk to commission a piece for eight choirs of five voices each. For 10 points, name this composer whose motet Ecce Beatam Lucem may have inspired Thomas Tallis to write Spem in Alium.

66315f61ddd20e7cbb31ddac.question: A composer with this first name accompanied Philip II on his travels throughout Europe, where he influenced English virginal composers with his variations on themes like canto del Caballero. A composer with this first name was called "the Devil dressed as a priest" for his controversial treatise The Key to Modulation. Most of the works of a composer with this first name were posthumously published by his son Hernando; that blind organist had the surname de Cabezón. A composer with this first name wrote six concertos for two organs and around (*) 150 keyboard sonatas. Most of Bach's Weimar concertos transcribe pieces by a composer with this first name for keyboard. Composers with this first name include one surnamed Soler and another who wrote a C major mandolin concerto for a prodigy he taught at an all-girls school. For 10 points, give this first name of the director of the Ospedale della Pietà.
66315f61ddd20e7cbb31ddac.question_sanitized: A composer with this first name accompanied Philip II on his travels throughout Europe, where he influenced English virginal composers with his variations on themes like canto del Caballero. A composer with this first name was called "the Devil dressed as a priest" for his controversial treatise The Key to Modulation. Most of the works of a composer with this first name were posthumously published by his son Hernando; that blind organist had the surname de Cabezon. A composer with this first name wrote six concertos for two organs and around (*) 150 keyboard sonatas. Most of Bach's Weimar concertos transcribe pieces by a composer with this first name for keyboard. Composers with this first name include one surnamed Soler and another who wrote a C major mandolin concerto for a prodigy he taught at an all-girls school. For 10 points, give this first name of the director of the Ospedale della Pieta.
66315f61ddd20e7cbb31ddac.answer: Antonio [accept Antonio de Cabezón; accept Antonio Soler; accept Antonio Vivaldi]
66315f61ddd20e7cbb31ddac.answer_sanitized: Antonio [accept Antonio de Cabezon; accept Antonio Soler; accept Antonio Vivaldi]

66315f61ddd20e7cbb31ddb0.question: Dominique Vellard founded an ensemble named for this composer that recorded Liszt's little-known responsory Septem Sacramenta in 2022. The speaker of a song by this composer "will carry nothing but black as long as [they] can return to you." A song by this composer opens with the melody: pickup E - G, E - G, F - D, C. Erwin Panofsky claimed that the painting Léal Souvenir depicts this composer, although this is disputed. This composer wrote the songs Mon seul et souverain désir and De (*) plus en plus. This composer's death likely inspired the lament Mort, tu es navré de ton dart. This second most famous composer to be praised for adopting the contenance angloise was likely the teacher of Johannes Ockeghem. For 10 points, name this composer who, along with Busnois, Tapissier, and Du Fay, was a member of the Burgundian school.
66315f61ddd20e7cbb31ddb0.question_sanitized: Dominique Vellard founded an ensemble named for this composer that recorded Liszt's little-known responsory Septem Sacramenta in 2022. The speaker of a song by this composer "will carry nothing but black as long as [they] can return to you." A song by this composer opens with the melody: pickup E - G, E - G, F - D, C. Erwin Panofsky claimed that the painting Leal Souvenir depicts this composer, although this is disputed. This composer wrote the songs Mon seul et souverain desir and De (*) plus en plus. This composer's death likely inspired the lament Mort, tu es navre de ton dart. This second most famous composer to be praised for adopting the contenance angloise was likely the teacher of Johannes Ockeghem. For 10 points, name this composer who, along with Busnois, Tapissier, and Du Fay, was a member of the Burgundian school.
66315f61ddd20e7cbb31ddb0.answer: Gilles Binchois [accept Gilles de Bins; accept Ensemble Gilles Binchois] (Léal Souvenir is by Jan van Eyck.)
66315f61ddd20e7cbb31ddb0.answer_sanitized: Gilles Binchois [accept Gilles de Bins; accept Ensemble Gilles Binchois] (Leal Souvenir is by Jan van Eyck.)
